t: 8.1 General principles (AASM 2021 · European Insomnia Guideline 2023 · NICE 2025)
CBT-I — Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia FIRST LINE
— gold standard in chronic insomnia; superior long-term efficacy to pharmacotherapy (Mitchell M.D. BMC Fam Pract 2012 meta-analysis). 5 main components:
Sleep restriction (Spielman) — restricting time in bed to equal sleep time;
Cognitive therapy — catastrophic thoughts about sleep;
Digital CBT-I (Sleepio, SHUTi) — effect close to clinical format; NICE 2025 — cost-effective alternative.
Pharmacotherapy — short-term (2–4 weeks), when CBT-I is non-responsive or unavailable:
Newer — suvorexant, lemborexant (orexin antagonists) — less dependence;
Melatonin — for circadian component or in elderly; ramelteon (melatonin agonist).
In older adults — Beers Criteria; avoid benzodiazepines and antihistamines (diphenhydramine).
Comorbid depression — sedative antidepressant (mirtazapine, trazodone) — long-term use side effects should be considered.
AASM Clinical Practice Guideline for Behavioral and Psychological Treatments (Edinger J.D. 2021).
European Insomnia Guideline (Riemann D. J Sleep Res 2023).
NICE 2025 — CBT-I in all age groups.
CBT for insomnia (CBT-I — Cognitive Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia), 5 components
— Stimulus control, sleep restriction, cognitive therapy, sleep hygiene, relaxation.
Insomnia Severity Index (ISI — Insomnia Severity Index)
Without treatment, chronic course and comorbidity (MDD, anxiety).
Myth 1: "Sleep medications are the main treatment for insomnia"
AASM 2021 — CBT-I first-line; pharmacotherapy short-term adjunct; long-term use tolerance, dependence.
Alcohol reduces REM, causes midnight awakenings, leads to chronic insomnia.
Myth 3: "Antihistamine (diphenhydramine) is safe in the elderly"
Beers Criteria — antihistamines in the elderly: anticholinergic side effects (falls, cognitive decline); avoid.
Myth 4: "Valerian, kava are safe alternatives for insomnia"
valerian Cochrane (Leach 2015) — evidence for effect insufficient; kava hepatotoxicity.
Myth 5: "8 hours of sleep is necessary for everyone"
Individual need 6–9 hours; important is daytime function.
Myth 6: "Waking up during the night is harmful"
Brief awakenings are normal; stimulus control — rise if you can't sleep for 20 min.
Edinger J.D. et al. Behavioral and psychological treatments for chronic insomnia disorder in adults: an AASM clinical practice guideline. J Clin Sleep Med 2021;17(2):255–262.
Riemann D. et al. The European Insomnia Guideline 2023. J Sleep Res 2023;32(6):e14035.
Mitchell M.D. et al. Comparative effectiveness of cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia: a systematic review. BMC Fam Pract 2012;13:40.
Buysse D.J. et al. Efficacy of brief behavioral treatment for chronic insomnia in older adults. Arch Intern Med 2011;171(10):887–895.
